title: Complexity, Profiling, Rendering Budgets, and Responsiveness · Behavior Case: Event Latency Budget
summary: Optimization starts from intuition, solver and drawing time are combined, huge mazes freeze the event loop, and average latency hides stalls. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the event latency budget increment in representative performance and responsiveness report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define response, animation, cancellation, and redraw budgets and report percentiles or worst representative stalls.
Measure generation, solving, state conversion, drawing, callback delay, startup, save, and package behavior separately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 5182 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate algorithm, render, and cancellation profiles under a representative responsiveness budget; the prior good state must survive failure; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. A defensible solution applies Event Latency Budget, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to representative performance and responsiveness report.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-performance-profiling-5
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-performance-profiling-5.
# Case 9036: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def worked_maze_performance_profiling_5(cells: int = 4096, solver_ms: int = 18, render_ms: int = 26, cancellation_ms: int = 41, event_budget_ms: int = 50) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "gridworked5",
        "topology9036",
        "search5dekrow",
        "eventworked59036",
        "access9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or min(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms, event_budget_ms) < 0:
        return False, cells, "profile-shape"
    if solver_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, solver_ms, "solver-budget"
    if render_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, render_ms, "render-budget"
    if cancellation_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, cancellation_ms, "cancel-budget"
    assert max(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms) <= event_budget_ms
    return True, max(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms), "responsive-capacity-profile"

# Evidence: maze-event-latency-budget-6703km

def evidence_maze_event_latency_budget_6703km(cells: int = 4096, solver_ms: int = 18, render_ms: int = 26, cancellation_ms: int = 41, event_budget_ms: int = 50) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:

        "grid6703km",
        "topology8627",
        "searchmk3076",
        "event6703km8627",
        "access86276703km",
        "recoverymk30768627",

    if cells < 1 or min(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms, event_budget_ms) < 0:

    if solver_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, solver_ms, "solver-budget"
    if render_ms > event_budget_ms:

    if cancellation_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, cancellation_ms, "cancel-budget"
    assert max(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms) <= event_budget_ms
    return True, max(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms), "responsive-capacity-profile"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define response, animation, cancellation, and redraw budgets and report percentiles or worst representative stalls.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Event Latency Budget model · Theory Event Latency Budg…, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Event Latency Budget precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Event Latency Budget model · Theory Event Latency Budg…, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 1972 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate algorithm, render, and cancellation profiles under a representative responsiveness budget; the prior good state must survive failure; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates.
